stem: A school principal surveyed 40 students selected at random from the 800 students enrolled at her school. Of those surveyed, 30 said they preferred a longer lunch period. Based on this survey, which of the following is the best estimate of the number of students in the school who prefer a longer lunch period?
explanation: Because the 40 students were selected at random, the sample is representative of the full school population of 800. The proportion who prefer a longer lunch period in the sample is 30/40 = 3/4. Applying this proportion to the full population: (3/4) × 800 = 600. The best estimate is 600 students.

distractorRationale.A: Confusing sample with population — treating the sample count of 30 as if it directly represents the whole school, ignoring that the sample is only 40 of 800 students.
distractorRationale.B: Overgeneralizing scope — a student might divide 800 by 40 to get 20 groups, then multiply 20 by the wrong reference number (perhaps guessing 15 as half of 30), arriving at 300; or they may only scale by a factor of 10 instead of 20.
distractorRationale.D: Sample size misapplication — a student might reason that since 30 out of 40 is a high proportion, nearly all of the 800 students must prefer it, and approximate 750 (i.e., 800 × 30/32 or simply guessing a high number without careful proportion work).
